summary.intent: Perform a full audit of the navigation category agnostic components to close Epic #223, ensuring spec conformance, complete proptest coverage, and separation of monolithic test files into per-component modules.
summary.outcome: The audit was completed with all gates green: test reorg landed, 6 spec files synced to the correct shipped impl, Steps S1 behavior corrected, TreeView §5/§6 variants (lazy-loading + renamable) fully implemented via TDD, new Link + NavigationMenu proptests added, and the LoadChildrenFn Rc design replaced with the workspace-standard Callback type. First commit (C1, test reorg) was landed; C2–C5 and the PR are pending.

repo: The `input/` directory (e.g. tests/proptest_state_machines/input/) is the canonical blueprint for per-component test layout: shared strategies in mod.rs, each component owns a <component>.rs sibling.

The `EffectName: Copy` bound on Machine::Effect prevents carrying payload in Effect variants (e.g. LoadChildren(Key) is impossible); payload must travel via transient Context fields or — better — a Callback Prop.

Adapter notification callbacks follow a strict convention: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(...) + Send + Sync>> Prop + builder, fired by the Effect closure. Rc<dyn Fn> violates the Send+Sync requirement of the Service's StoredValue/Signal storage.

The spec-snippet harness (crates/xtask/src/spec/compile_snippets.rs) does only syn::parse_file syntax validation, not type/borrow checking; blocks can opt out with rust,no_check. Spec code edits carry low breakage risk.
Spec sections labeled 'Variant:' (e.g. tree-view.md §5/§6) were intentionally out of scope for the original implementation issue (#267), which only covered §1 state machine and §2 ConnectApi.

The proptest-regressions/ file is auto-seeded on test failures and must be manually reverted if the test invariant (not the impl) was wrong — otherwise it will cause spurious reproductions on future runs.
Items marked closed in sub-issues of an Epic do not block closing the Epic itself; the Epic close is a deliberate act after merge + CI + Codex review.

code.finding: Last-step NextStep handling: original impl left final step Current and only fired Effect::Complete. Spec says it should mark the step Complete AND fire the effect. Changed per user decision.
Test next_step_completion_preserves_current_status was pinning the old (incorrect) last-step behavior; updated alongside the impl change.
TreeItemConfig lacked Clone+PartialEq derives; they were required for it to ride in the Clone+PartialEq Event enum (ChildrenLoaded payload). Added as part of the TreeView §5 implementation.

LoadChildrenFn (Rc<dyn Fn(Key)>) was dead code — referenced only in its own definition. Replaced with on_load_children: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(Key) + Send + Sync>> Prop, eliminating the pending_load transient Context field entirely.
TreeItemConfig derives were stale in the foundation spec after adding Clone+PartialEq to the impl. Phase 2 audit caught this and synced it.
Initial invariant 'pressed iff State::Pressed' was too strong — Press then Focus leaves pressed=true in State::Focused mid-press. Correct invariant: Pressed implies pressed, Idle implies !pressed.

Initial invariant 'open_item must be in registered items' failed because item_is_registered is intentionally permissive before the adapter's first SetItems call. Must be guarded on items_registered, mirroring the tabs proptest pattern.

workflow: Dispatching 3–6 parallel drift-audit agents (one per component or component group) then doing mechanical work in the foreground dramatically compresses audit wall-clock time.
Cross-checking agent spec-sync output by grepping for referenced symbol names in the impl (not re-reading the full diff) is an efficient fidelity check against fabrication.
When proptests find counterexamples during authoring, the failure is almost always an over-strong invariant, not an impl bug — root-cause before fixing.

Commit sequencing for interleaved mechanical + feature work requires temporarily reverting files to their pre-feature state for early commits, saving full versions to /tmp, then restoring for the feature commit.
Auto-staging by hooks means git reset is required before manually building per-commit staging sets.

friction: TreeItemConfig missing Clone+PartialEq was a blocking compile error discovered only when the ChildrenLoaded event payload was wired in; required an additive change in ars-collections.
The EffectName: Copy constraint meant Effect::LoadChildren could not carry a Key, forcing a two-step design (transient pending_load field) that was architecturally inferior and later replaced.

The initial TreeView agent copied LoadChildrenFn = Rc<dyn Fn(Key)> verbatim from a self-contradictory spec section; user review caught it, requiring a full refactor pass across impl, tests, core spec, and both adapter specs.
Git hooks auto-staged all worktree changes, requiring a full reset and manual per-commit staging dance for the 5-commit narrative.
The proptest-regressions/ file got contaminated by seeds from debugging runs on incorrect invariants and had to be identified and manually reverted.

Commit C1 (test reorg) conflicted with C4 (TreeView variants) because the reorg tree_view.rs files already contained variant additions; required reverting test files to pre-variant state, committing C1, then restoring for C4.
open_items: Commits C2–C5 still need to be created (spec syncs, Steps S1, TreeView variants, new proptests) and pushed to GitHub.
PR targeting main with 'Closes #223' needs to be opened; the snapshot-reviewed label must be added (one new .snap fixture: tree_view_node_rename_input).
Codex review loop must run to 👍 before Epic #223 can be closed.
Epic #223 should only be closed after merge + green CI + Codex approval — not at PR creation.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for TreeView lazy-loading (Effect::LoadChildren wiring) and renamable nodes do not yet exist; specs are updated but no adapter code was written.
MD060 table-alignment lint warnings in adapter specs (leptos/dioxus tree-view.md) were left unresolved — pre-existing cosmetic issue, not a CI gate.
